## [00:00 - 03:06]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about your webinar's tone. So what is tone? Well besides, it's also the general character or attitude you wish to portray in your webinar. So in other words, it's how you come across. So let me just give you some options that this is what I'm thinking of when I say tone. So in your webinar, you can be enthusiastic. You can be informative. You can be caring. You can be sharing. You can be scientific. You can be confident. And ideally, you want to be all of these in your presentation style so that you can attract and resonate with as much of your audience as possible. The problem is, it's tough to prioritize those because you probably can't be every one of those all the way through. So which one or ones are most important for you? So when I say important for you, you got several things you got to look at. First one is what's your natural personality style? Are you someone who is like, I have information. I'm going to present it to you or hey, I really care and I want to share this with you. Okay, so there's there's two different ways of doing this. And one tends to rule over the other in your natural style. So one of these elements is what's your natural style? The second element is what is your audience going to respond best to? So if you are in the. I'll just call them softer skills area. ## [03:06 - 06:08]

So I have a friend who's actually almost exactly the opposite of me and we make a great team because her number one thing is caring and sharing. She's pretty informative and she's very confident, but enthusiasm and scientific are lower on her list. Okay, so as you can see, you can be different things and of course you're going to be different things at different times in your presentation because all these factors are going to be reflected in your presentation. But what are they going to be the predominant styles that you want to use and I recommend you actually consciously choose so that you can create a consistent image in all of your presentations. And I recognize that you choose a style that the majority of your target audience will respond to positively. Realize when I gave you that example of the softer skills versus the harder skills, I made a vast generalization. So even if I'm teaching hard skills like how to get more traffic to your page and how to build a sales page that converts and those things, presumably harder skills, you can teach hard skills from a more caring and sharing than a informative confidence side. Likewise, in the soft skills area, you can teach enthusiastic and confident and you can teach caring and sharing. That makes sense. So in each one of these areas, there is a segment of people who are going to be attracted to you because of the way that you present, no matter what it is that you present. I do encourage you and warn you that whatever you choose, you are going to drive some people away and attract others. So there is a person who has a presence in the online marketing space right now, who's very rough, very course. You can just tell from my tone of voice, this is not my person. I actually don't even know his name because he repels me. He's driven me away, but there is a group of people in the online marketing world who are drawn to this guy like a magnet. They love him. It's just not me. And so realize that whatever you choose, you are going to attract some and repel others. And so I encourage you to as you're making this conscious choice to make sure that the audience that you are creating and that you're going to attract with that style is the tribe you want to buy your product and that you want to hang out with over time.

## [06:08 - 06:34]

Because ultimately the people who buy your product are probably the people who are going to eventually buy your higher end product, maybe doing one on one coaching or group coaching or masterminds, etc. With you and you are potentially going to spend a lot of time with the people you attract into your audience. So just make sure that you actually work to attract the kind of people who you really want to hang out with long term.
